Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities recorded as base ten numerals as well as rounding these to given decimal places and significant figures.
Suggested Topic Name: Place Value, Large Numbers, and Rounding
Alternative Labels: Large Numbers; Significant Figures and Decimal Places
Notes / Interpretation: Includes modelling, comparing and ordering numbers above one billion, rounding whole numbers and decimals, significant figures, and decimal places.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties used to solve problems
Alternative Labels: Mental Calculation Strategies; Number Properties for Mental Math
Notes / Interpretation: Focuses on multiplying/dividing by powers of 10 and applying mental strategies and number properties.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of (i) whole numbers, and (ii) decimal numbers, to solve problems.
Short Label: Basic Operations with Whole Numbers and Decimals
Notes / Interpretation: Covers addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of whole numbers and decimals in real-life problems.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding and the use of powers of natural numbers in solving problems.
Alternative Labels: Repeated Factors; HCF and LCM with Prime Factors
Notes / Interpretation: Moves from repeated factors and index notation to HCF and LCM applications.

Official Curriculum Description: Simplify, compare and order a mixture of positive fractions (i.e. common, percent and decimal) by changing all to equivalent (i) fractions (ii) decimals, or (iii) percentages.
Notes / Interpretation: About equivalence, comparison and ordering across common fractions, decimals and percentages.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of the process of addition and/or subtraction of fractions and apply this in solving problems
Notes / Interpretation: Covers addition and subtraction of unlike and mixed fractions in context.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of the process of multiplying and dividing positive fractions and apply this in solving problems
Notes / Interpretation: Includes multiplying/dividing fractions and finding fractions of quantities.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of the concept of ratios and its relationship to fractions and use it to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning
Notes / Interpretation: Combines ratio language, unit rates, equivalent ratios, proportions and percent of quantity.

Official Curriculum Description: Derive the rule for a set of points of a relation, draw a table of values to graph the relation in a number plane and make predictions about subsequent elements of the relation.
Alternative Labels: Function Rules and Graphs; Relations in the Coordinate Plane
Notes / Interpretation: Covers extending patterns, describing rules, using tables, plotting points and interpreting simple relations.

Official Curriculum Description: Simplify algebraic expressions involving the four basic operations and substituting values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
Notes / Interpretation: Focuses on creating, simplifying, operating on and evaluating algebraic expressions.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of linear equations of the form x + a = b (where a and b are integers) by modelling problems as a linear equation and solving the problems concretely, pictorially, and symbolically.
Alternative Labels: One-Variable Linear Equations; Story Problems as Linear Equations
Notes / Interpretation: Includes translating word problems to equations and solving them concretely, pictorially and symbolically.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding of angles including adjacent, vertically opposite, complementary, supplementary and use them to solve problems.
Alternative Labels: Adjacent, Complementary, Supplementary, and Vertically Opposite Angles
Notes / Interpretation: Deals with angle types and angle relationships used in solving problems.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate how to construct a perpendicular to a line from a given point, bisect a line, bisect angles, and construct angles of the following sizes: 30˚, 45˚, 60˚, 75˚ and 90˚
Suggested Topic Name: Geometric Constructions: Perpendiculars, Bisectors, and Standard Angles
Notes / Interpretation: A construction unit covering perpendiculars, bisectors and standard constructed angles.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate the ability to find the perimeter of plane shapes including circles using the concept of pi (π) to find the circumference of a circle.
Alternative Labels: Circumference of a Circle; Plane Perimeter
Notes / Interpretation: Focuses on perimeter of plane shapes and circumference of circles.

Official Curriculum Description: Derive the formula for determining the area of a triangle and use it to solve problems
Notes / Interpretation: Builds and applies the formula for the area of a triangle.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding of bearings, vector and its components using real life cases.
Alternative Labels: Scalar and Vector Quantities; Component and Magnitude-Bearing Form
Notes / Interpretation: Introduces bearings, scalar vs vector quantities, vector components, magnitude and direction.

Official Curriculum Description: Perform a single transformation (i.e. reflection and translation) on a 2D shape using graph paper (including technology) and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence, similarity, etc.)
Alternative Labels: Transformations on the Coordinate Plane; Symmetry and Transformation
Notes / Interpretation: Covers reflectional symmetry, reflection rules, translations, and verifying congruence/similarity.

Official Curriculum Description: Select, justify, and use appropriate methods to collect data (quantitative and qualitative), display and analyse the data (grouped/ungrouped) presented in frequency tables, line graphs, pie graphs, bar graphs or pictographs and use these to solve and/or pose problems.
Alternative Labels: Surveys, Tables, and Graphs; Collecting and Displaying Data
Notes / Interpretation: Focuses on choosing methods of data collection and presenting data in tables and common charts.

Official Curriculum Description: Determine the measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) for a given ungrouped data and use it to solve problems
Suggested Topic Name: Mean, Median, and Mode of Ungrouped Data
Notes / Interpretation: Computes and interprets central tendency for ungrouped data.

Official Curriculum Description: Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving single events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems
Notes / Interpretation: Introduces likelihood and probability for single events.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities in standard form and rounding numbers and decimals to significant figures and a given number of decimal places
Suggested Topic Name: Large Numbers, Standard Form, and Rounding
Alternative Labels: Scientific Notation (school-level standard form); Place Value
Notes / Interpretation: Includes reading/writing large numbers, standard form, and rounding to significant figures/decimal places.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply the concepts and vocabulary of sets on sets of factors of numbers to identify perfect squares, determine their square root and solve real life problems involving union and intersection of two sets
Suggested Topic Name: Sets, Perfect Squares, Square Roots, and Set Operations
Notes / Interpretation: Uses sets to identify perfect squares, determine square roots, and solve union/intersection problems.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties used to solve problems
Notes / Interpretation: Continuation of mental-math fluency and number-property strategies.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply the understanding of the addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of (i) whole numbers within 10,000, and (ii) decimals up to 1/1000, to solve problems and round answers to given decimal places.
Notes / Interpretation: Extends operations to larger whole numbers and decimals, including real-life problems.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding and the use of the laws of indices in solving problems (including real life problems) involving powers of natural numbers
Suggested Topic Name: Laws of Indices and Exponential Relationships
Notes / Interpretation: Covers laws of indices, simplification, exponential equations, and exponential growth contexts.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply the understanding of operation on fractions to solve problems involving fractions of given quantities and round the results to given decimal and significant places
Suggested Topic Name: Operations on Fractions and Fraction Problem Solving
Alternative Labels: BODMAS with Fractions; Fractions in Context
Notes / Interpretation: Reviews and extends operations on fractions, including order of operations and word problems.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of ratio, rate and proportions and use it these to solve real-world mathematical problems
Suggested Topic Name: Ratio, Rate, Proportion, and Speed Graphs
Alternative Labels: Unit Rate and Speed; Proportional Relationships
Notes / Interpretation: Combines unit conversions, unit rates, speed/distance-time graphs, and proportional relationships.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate the ability to draw table of values for a linear relation, graph the relation in a number plane, determine the gradient of the line and use it to write equation of a line of the form y = mx + c.
Suggested Topic Name: Linear Graphs, Gradient, and Equation of a Line
Alternative Labels: Slope and Graphing; y = mx + c
Notes / Interpretation: Focuses on gradient, graphing linear relations, and writing equations of lines.

Official Curriculum Description: Solve problems involving algebraic expressions (including multiplication of binomial expressions) factorise given expressions and substitute values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
Suggested Topic Name: Expanding, Simplifying, Factorising, and Evaluating Algebraic Expressions
Alternative Labels: Binomials and Factorisation; Algebraic Fractions and Substitution
Notes / Interpretation: Includes expansion, binomial multiplication, algebraic fractions, substitution, and factorisation.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of linear inequalities of the form x + a ≥ b (where a and b are integers) by modelling problems as a linear inequalities and solving the problems concretely, pictorially, and symbolically.
Notes / Interpretation: Translates, solves, and finds solution sets for simple linear inequalities.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding and use of the relationship between parallel lines and alternate and corresponding angles and use the sum of angles in a triangle to deduce the angle sum in any polygon.
Suggested Topic Name: Parallel Lines and Angle Sum in Polygons
Alternative Labels: Alternate and Corresponding Angles; Interior Angle Sum
Notes / Interpretation: Uses parallel-line angle facts and derives angle sums in polygons.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate the ability to perform geometric constructions of the angles (75˚, 105˚, 60˚, 135˚ and 150˚), and construct triangles and find locus of points under given conditions.
Suggested Topic Name: Geometric Constructions of Angles, Triangles, and Loci
Notes / Interpretation: Construction unit covering advanced angles, triangles, and loci.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply the Pythagoras theorem, the primary trigonometric ratios and the formulas for determining the area of a circle to solve real problems.
Suggested Topic Name: Area of Circles, Pythagoras, and Basic Trigonometry
Alternative Labels: Right-Triangle Trigonometry; Angles of Elevation and Depression
Notes / Interpretation: Combines circle area, Pythagorean theorem, and primary trig ratios.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding of addition and subtraction of vectors and their applications in solving basic problems
Suggested Topic Name: Vector Addition, Subtraction, and Scalar Multiplication
Notes / Interpretation: Applies vector operations in component form.

Official Curriculum Description: Perform a single transformation (i.e. rotation) on a 2D shape using graph paper (including technology) and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence)
Alternative Labels: Rotational Transformation; Rotations on the Coordinate Plane
Notes / Interpretation: Introduces rotation and uses it to verify congruence.

Official Curriculum Description: Select, justify, and use appropriate methods to collect data (quantitative and qualitative), use the data (grouped/ungrouped) to construct and interpret frequency tables, bar charts, pie charts, and pictograms to solve and/or pose problems.
Suggested Topic Name: Types of Data and Data Representation
Alternative Labels: Grouped/Ungrouped Data; Stem-and-Leaf, Waffle, and Charts
Notes / Interpretation: Classifies data and represents it using varied charts and plots.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and range for grouped data and explain when it’s most appropriate to use the mean, median, or mode.
Suggested Topic Name: Measures of Central Tendency for Grouped/Ungrouped Data
Notes / Interpretation: Computes central tendency and range and justifies which measure best fits a context.

Official Curriculum Description: Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving two independent events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems.
Notes / Interpretation: Deals with sample spaces and probabilities for two independent events.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply the understanding of place value in solving real life problems involving integers of any size, rounding this to given decimal places and significant figures
Suggested Topic Name: Significant Figures, Decimal Places, and Place-Value Problems
Alternative Labels: Significant Figures; Real-Life Place Value Problems
Notes / Interpretation: Applies place value, standard form, significant figures and decimal places in real-life problems.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between members of the rational number system and solve real life problems involving union and intersection of three sets
Notes / Interpretation: Uses sets to show relationships among number-system subsets and solve union/intersection problems.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply mental mathematics and properties to determine answers for addition and subtraction of basic facts.
Notes / Interpretation: The PDF’s wording and indicators are slightly inconsistent here; operationally this is a mental math and number properties unit.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply the understanding of addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of decimal numbers to solve problems, and round answers to given decimal places and significant figures
Suggested Topic Name: Decimal Operations and Multi-Step Word Problems
Notes / Interpretation: Applies the four basic operations in multi-step real-life problems and rounds answers.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding of surds as real numbers, the process of adding and subtracting of surds as well as determining (using a calculator) the approximate square root of a number that is not a perfect square.
Alternative Labels: Radical Expressions; Square Roots of Non-Perfect Squares
Notes / Interpretation: Introduces simple/compound surds, rules of surds, simplification, and approximating square roots.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply the understanding of operations on fractions to solve problems involving fractions of given quantities and round the results to given decimal and significant places
Suggested Topic Name: Fractions, Percentages, and Fraction Word Problems
Notes / Interpretation: Reviews and applies fraction operations and percentage contexts.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply the understanding of ratio, rate and proportions to solve problems that involve rates, ratios, and proportional reasoning and use it to solve real- world mathematical problems.
Suggested Topic Name: Proportional Reasoning in Finance and Real-Life Contexts
Alternative Labels: Percent Applications; Simple Interest, VAT, Discount, SSNIT
Notes / Interpretation: Uses proportional reasoning in practical finance settings including tax, commission, depreciation, insurance, NHIL and SSNIT.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate the ability to construct tables of values for pairs of linear relations, graph the relations in a number plane and determine the intersection of the lines to solve simultaneous linear equations.
Notes / Interpretation: Constructs tables and graphs of paired linear relations and uses intersections to solve simultaneous equations.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate an understanding of (i) change of subject (ii) substituting values to evaluate expressions, and (iii) factorize expressions that have simple binomial as a factor.
Suggested Topic Name: Change of Subject, Substitution, and Factorisation
Alternative Labels: Making the Subject of a Formula; Evaluating Formulae
Notes / Interpretation: Covers changing the subject of a formula, substitution into formulae, and simple binomial factorisation.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding of single variable linear inequalities with rational coefficients including: • solving inequalities • verifying • comparing • graphing
Notes / Interpretation: Solves, verifies and graphs single-variable linear inequalities with rational coefficients.

Official Curriculum Description: Apply the properties of angles at a point, angles on a straight line, vertically opposite angles, corresponding, angles to` solve problems.
Suggested Topic Name: Polygon Angle Sums, Similarity, and Congruence
Notes / Interpretation: Applies angle-sum formulas and triangle similarity/congruence.

Official Curriculum Description: Construct inscribed and circumscribed triangles and parallelograms with given dimensions.
Notes / Interpretation: Construction unit spanning inscribed/circumscribed circles and quadrilateral constructions.

Official Curriculum Description: Derive the formulas for determining the surface area of prisms (i.e. cuboid and triangular prism) and use to solve problems.
Suggested Topic Name: Surface Area of Prisms and Position Vectors
Alternative Labels: Nets of Prisms; Position Vectors on the Cartesian Plane
Notes / Interpretation: This official standard mixes two ideas in the PDF: surface area of prisms and position vectors.

Official Curriculum Description: Solve problems involving bearings and addition/subtraction of vectors
Alternative Labels: Parallel/Perpendicular Vectors; Triangle and Parallelogram Laws
Notes / Interpretation: Applies vector ideas to bearings, vector relationships, and vector addition/resolution.

Official Curriculum Description: Demonstrate understanding of how to perform an enlargement on a geometrical shape given a scale factor and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence, similarity, etc.)
Notes / Interpretation: Covers enlargement by scale factor and its similarity properties.

Official Curriculum Description: Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data (grouped/ungrouped), use the data to construct and interpret frequency tables and histogram and use it to determine the mode and to solve and/or pose problems.
Suggested Topic Name: Frequency Tables, Histograms, and Modal Class
Notes / Interpretation: Uses grouped data, histograms and other displays to identify modal classes and interpret data.

Official Curriculum Description: Select, justify, and use appropriate methods of collecting data (quantitative and qualitative), organise and analyse the data (grouped/ungrouped) to interpret the results using the descriptive statistics (measures of central tendency and range).
Suggested Topic Name: Data Collection Bias and Descriptive Statistics
Notes / Interpretation: Highlights bias in data collection and applies descriptive statistics to interpret results.

Official Curriculum Description: Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving two dependent events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems.
Notes / Interpretation: Deals with sample spaces and probabilities for dependent events.
